content_markdown:
The diagram below shows the relation between the pressure and the volume of the gas in an engine for one cycle of operation of the engine.

Work is done by the gas in the engine during

diagram_metadata.description: A pressure-volume (P-V) graph showing a four-stage cycle: Q to R (isobaric expansion), R to S (expansion), S to T (isochoric cooling/pressure drop), and T back to Q (compression).

vibe_explanation: Work is done by a gas when it expands, which corresponds to an increase in volume ($ΔV > 0$). Looking at the P-V diagram, the volume increases during the processes $Q \rightarrow R$ and $R \rightarrow S$. During $S \rightarrow T$, the volume is constant (no work), and during $T \rightarrow Q$, the volume decreases (work is done on the gas).
